etymology.description: 人(人) (rén, "person") + 牛(牛) (niú, "cow") = items or parts related to or involving work with animals -> item; component; piece; part
etymology.interpretation: The combination of 人(人) (rén, "person") and 牛(牛) (niú, "cow") suggests activities or tasks a person might perform with cattle, illustrating the idea of a task or work unit, and thus extending to mean an 'item' or 'component' as a distinct piece of work or object.

examples.chinese: 这是一件漂亮的衬衫(這是一件漂亮的襯衫)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè shì yī jiàn piàoliang de chènshān.

examples.chinese: 他每周都会买一件新衣服(他每週都會買一件新衣服)。
examples.pinyin: Tā měi zhōu dōu huì mǎi yī jiàn xīn yīfú.
examples.english: He buys a new piece of clothing every week.

examples.chinese: 那是一个重要的项目的一部分(那是一個重要的項目的一部分)。
examples.pinyin: Nà shì yīgè zhòngyào de xiàngmù de yī bùfèn.
examples.english: That is a part of an important project.
